name: Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo — circular from Espiel
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Espiel traverse the mountainous terrain of the Guadiato Valley within the Sierra Morena region of Córdoba, Spain. The landscape features holm oaks and dense undergrowth, offering varied routes from gentle stretches to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains. Cyclists can expect roads with smooth tarmac in some areas, providing views across vast, undulating green vistas. Best road cycling routes around Espiel The most popular road cycling route is Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete, circling the prominent Puente Nuevo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo — circular from Espiel, a moderate 54.8 miles (88.3 km) path. This circular route connects municipalities and offers diverse scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Assuan-Trasierra Road – Artafi Pass - Trassierra loop from Villaharta, a 64.6 miles (103.9 km) trail leading through the Artafi Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Espiel is defined by mountainous scenery, holm oak forests, and the Guadiato River valley. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Espiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Espiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Espiel?
A: Road cycling routes around Espiel traverse the mountainous terrain of the Guadiato Valley within the Sierra Morena region. You'll encounter varied landscapes, from areas with smooth tarmac to some gravel sections, all set against a backdrop of holm oaks and dense undergrowth. Expect significant elevation gains on many routes, offering challenging climbs and rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels in Espiel?
A: Yes, Espiel offers a range of road cycling routes. While many feature challenging climbs and are rated as 'difficult', there are also 'moderate' options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016945594/penarroja-pueblonuevo-circular-from-espiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo — circular from Espiel&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route. The region's diverse terrain ensures there's something for various abilities.

Q: What are some of the natural attractions or scenic viewpoints I can find along road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural beauty. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010631721/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; circles a significant natural feature. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadanuño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3121300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra Morena region itself is known for its dense forests and rolling hills, providing breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see while road cycling in Espiel?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. The Vía Verde del Guadiato y Los Pedroches, an old mining railway line, is a notable feature, showcasing industrial heritage and centennial mining shafts. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Mozarabe de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; or the religious building of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1219085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Calixto&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the area's past.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Espiel?
A: Espiel's climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Espiel?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010631721/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride around the reservoir. Another highly-rated option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016945594/penarroja-pueblonuevo-circular-from-espiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo — circular from Espiel&lt;/a&gt;, connecting various municipalities with diverse scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Espiel?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the mountainous scenery, the challenging climbs, and the varied routes that cater to different preferences. The picturesque backdrops of holm oaks and vast green vistas are frequently highlighted as a major draw.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling routes in Espiel?
A: Absolutely. Espiel offers several routes that are ideal for longer distance rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Assuan-Trasierra Road – Artafi Pass - Trassierra loop from Villaharta&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 km and includes significant elevation, providing a substantial challenge for experienced cyclists looking for an extended adventure.

Q: Can I find routes that are less challenging or more accessible for road cycling in Espiel?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, the region also offers more accessible options. The Vía Verde del Guadiato y Los Pedroches, a repurposed old mining railway line, provides a relatively flat and accessible path suitable for non-motorized activities, offering a peaceful cycling experience away from traffic.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Espiel?
A: There are nearly 20 dedicated road cycling routes around Espiel, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the mountainous terrain and scenic landscapes of the Guadiato Valley.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest like springs or natural monuments along the routes?
A: Yes, you can discover several natural points of interest. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malos Pasos Spring&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2225250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Cordel&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6218520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, which are highlights in the area and often found near cycling paths.

Q: Is Espiel a good destination for advanced road cyclists seeking challenging climbs?
A: Definitely. Espiel is characterized by mountainous scenery and significant elevation gains, making it an excellent destination for advanced road cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo – Obejo Water Fountain loop from Villaharta&lt;/a&gt; feature over 2000 meters of ascent, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.
tips: Villa located in the Valle del Guadiato region. It has one of the best sport climbing areas in Andalusia.
A beautiful 13th century castle stands majestically on the hill, like a lookout for the town of Belmez.

Town that was born with the union of two independent municipalities: Peñarroya and Pueblonuevo del Terrible, which grew based on the mining activity of the region in the 19th century. The industrial boom was driven mainly by the Peñarroya Mining and Metallurgical Society (SMMP). The old SMMP buildings were converted after their decline in the mid-20th century into public buildings, such as a health center or geriatric hospital.

Small and quiet town that until 1833 belonged to Extremadura. To get to know its nooks and crannies, the town council proposes to walk the Ruta del Agua (1 km) to discover a typical urban landscape of the Valley of Los Pedroches. The route begins at the Cruz de la Fuente Vieja, to continue through the Pozo de la Fuente Vieja, Pozo del Morconcillo, Fuente Plaza Doña María Josefa Fernández and ends at the Pozo del Verdinal, where there is a 19th century waterwheel. The route ends at the Peñalá corner.
